source_jobs.title: Senior Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
UniCourt is headquartered in Tustin, California. However, our team largely operates remotely across several time zones spanning across multiple countries. This position can work remotely and is anticipated to have periodic in-person connections with team members at conferences and off-sites around the United States.



About Us

Our company creates some of the world’s most cutting-edge software solutions for legal data. We solve difficult problems, work on innovative technology, and build world-class API platforms for people and enterprises to interact with legal data and other public data sets. With some of the best minds in the industry, we’re one of the most sought-after learning and career destinations in the world of legaltech. If you’re looking to work at a company with opportunities to forge your career path in legaltech and sales, UniCourt is the right place for you.



UniCourt is a Legal Data as a Service (LDaaS) company dedicated to unlocking the potential of court data for Am Law 200 firms and Fortune 500 companies. Delivered via the only API-first platform in the legal technology market, UniCourt provides real-time state and federal court data for legal analytics. Our proprietary technology turns disparate court records into accessible, actionable insights. The UniCourt Enterprise API is the gold standard in legal data delivery, powering data-driven revenue growth and client satisfaction strategies.



Position Summary

If you thrive on initiative and drive, relish the entirety of the sales journey—from identifying promising leads to comprehending their requirements, offering tailored solutions, and ultimately sealing deals—then you're the ideal candidate for this role. You excel at cultivating relationships with current and potential large law clients, including engaging with C-Suite executives. Your background includes substantial experience in selling enterprise B2B data solutions for legal tech.



This role defines the essence of a "hunter," requiring the Account Executive to strategize, initiate, negotiate, and expand API data subscriptions and our latest analytics SaaS platform. We are specifically seeking a candidate with proven, iterative experience in legal services, financial institutions, insurance providers, or fortune 500s. We seek a collaborative individual eager to work alongside adept professionals in sales, marketing, and product development.



Duties & Responsibilities

 - Take ownership of inbound prospecting efforts, actively identifying and engaging potential customers.

 - Utilize a proactive approach to initiate meaningful conversations and establish rapport with prospects.

 - Employ strategic tactics to educate prospects on solutions, fostering timely and forecasted closures aligned with company metrics and objectives.

 - Drive individual sales targets while contributing to overall team objectives.

 - Implement effective sales strategies to meet and exceed targets consistently.

 - Employ a consultative approach to thoroughly understand client needs and preferences.

 - Provide tailored recommendations based on a deep understanding of client requirements.

 - Foster strong, enduring relationships with clients through dedicated attention and service.

 - Collaborate effectively with team members throughout the sales process, from lead generation to post-sale support.

 - Assist in various stages of client engagement to ensure seamless service delivery.

 - Stay abreast of market trends and changes in the legal tech space to inform sales strategies.

 - Develop and maintain comprehensive knowledge of UniCourt's products and services, as well as competitor offerings.

 - Utilize product expertise to effectively address client needs and differentiate UniCourt in the market.

 - Represent UniCourt at industry trade shows, conferences, and events to expand network and generate leads.

 - Travel as necessary to attend key industry gatherings and promote UniCourt's solutions.

Education, Knowl

source_jobs.title: Associate Product Manager, Court Data and Alerts
source_jobs.description:
About Us

UniCourt is a Legal Data as a Service (LDaaS) company dedicated to unlocking the potential of court data for Am Law 200 firms and Fortune 500 companies. Delivered via the only API-first platform in the legal technology market, UniCourt provides real-time state and federal court data for legal analytics. Our proprietary technology turns disparate court records into accessible, actionable insights. The UniCourt Enterprise API is the gold standard in legal data delivery, powering data-driven revenue growth and client satisfaction strategies.

Our team largely operates remotely across multiple time zones and countries. If you're looking to work at a company with opportunities to forge your career path in legal tech, UniCourt is the right place for you.



Position Summary

We are seeking an Associate Product Manager, Court Data and Alerts, who will serve as a critical partner to the Senior Product Manager of Court Data, helping ensure the integrity, reliability, and scalability of UniCourt’s foundational court data platform.

This role is highly execution-oriented and operational in nature, combining product coordination, data investigation, engineering collaboration, and legal technology research. The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, technically curious, organized, and excited to help build systems that improve legal data accessibility and automation.

You will work cross-functionally with Product, Engineering, Legal, Operations, and Sales to support new court onboarding initiatives, maintain data quality standards, coordinate portal and integration efforts, and help drive the execution of UniCourt’s evolving automated intelligence engine.



Duties & Responsibilities

Data Integrity & System Monitoring

 - Investigate discrepancies, gaps, and issues involving court, case, and master data using Kibana and internal monitoring tools

 - Document findings and communicate issues to Product and Engineering leadership

 - Monitor internal tracking and alert systems to ensure timely identification and resolution of issues

 - Assist with troubleshooting broken portals, integrations, and data feeds

Product & Engineering Coordination

 - Coordinate with Engineering teams to track ETAs, issue resolution, and product deliverables

 - Schedule and facilitate meetings related to new products, features, and integrations

 - Support the Senior Product Manager with execution and follow-through on operational initiatives

 - Create and maintain product documentation, requirements, and bug tracking within Jira and Google Workspace

Court Data Research & Onboarding

 - Research court document availability, limitations, and coverage for internal stakeholders and Sales inquiries

 - Ensure new court portal opportunities are properly analyzed and prioritized within the Court Onboarding roadmap

 - Assist with outreach and follow-up communication with courts regarding data feeds and bulk extracts

 - Coordinate legal review and approvals related to court portals and bulk data contracts

Client & Operational Support

 - Triage requests for manual case imports from PACER and other supported courts

 - Assist with tracking operational costs associated with bots, subscriptions, and court data feeds

 - Help maintain organized workflows and operational processes across the Court Data team



Education, Knowledge, Skills & Abilities

 - Bachelor’s degree required; JD, legal background or Master of Library and Information Science (MLIS) degree strongly preferred

 - 1+ years of product management or product operations experience, preferably within legal technology

 - Familiarity with court systems, litigation workflows, or legal data platforms preferred

 - Strong organizational and project coordination skills

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills

 - Comfortable working cross-functionally with technical and non-technical teams

 - Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities with exceptional attention to detail

 - Experienc

source_jobs.title: Product Manager, Applications
source_jobs.description:
About Us

UniCourt is a Legal Data as a Service (LDaaS) company dedicated to unlocking the potential of court data for Am Law 200 firms and Fortune 500 companies. Delivered via the only API-first platform in the legal technology market, UniCourt provides real-time state and federal court data for legal analytics. Our proprietary technology turns disparate court records into accessible, actionable insights. The UniCourt Enterprise API is the gold standard in legal data delivery, powering data-driven revenue growth and client satisfaction strategies.

Our team largely operates remotely across multiple time zones and countries. If you're looking to work at a company with opportunities to forge your career path in legal tech, UniCourt is the right place for you.

 

Position Summary

We are seeking a thoughtful, execution-oriented Product Manager to help drive the development of UniCourt DART, a legal technology platform focused on docket research, analytics, and tracking. This role will work closely with engineering, design, leadership, and customers to define and deliver product initiatives that improve how legal professionals access, analyze, and act on litigation and court data.

The ideal candidate has experience leading cross-functional product initiatives in B2B SaaS environments and is comfortable operating in a fast-moving, highly collaborative environment. Legal tech experience and/or a law degree is strongly preferred.

 

Duties & Responsibilities

Product Strategy & Execution

 - Lead product initiatives across docketing, analytics, workflow, and platform capabilities

 - Translate customer and business needs into clear product requirements, user stories, and acceptance criteria

 - Collaborate with stakeholders to shape roadmap priorities, timelines, and execution plans

 - Drive product initiatives from early concept through launch and iteration

Cross-Functional Collaboration

 - Work closely with Engineering teams to define requirements, prioritize work, and deliver high-quality product releases

 - Partner with Design and Engineering to improve usability, scalability, and platform performance

 - Coordinate communication and alignment between Product, Engineering, Customer Success, Sales, and Leadership teams

 - Balance customer needs, technical constraints, and business priorities in product decision-making

Product Discovery & Analytics

 - Conduct customer interviews, usage analysis, and competitive research to identify opportunities and improve product direction

 - Define, monitor, and analyze key product metrics and usage insights

 - Help identify areas for workflow optimization and enhanced customer experience

 - Stay informed on legal technology trends, litigation workflows, and evolving customer needs

Platform & Enterprise Features

 - Support the development of billing, subscription, and usage-tracking functionality

 - Contribute to RBAC (role-based access control), permissions, and enterprise administration design decisions

 - Collaborate on API-related initiatives, structured data workflows, and enterprise platform functionality

 

Education, Knowledge, Skills & Abilities

Required

 - 3–5 years of product management experience, preferably within B2B SaaS environments

 - JD or other legal background strongly preferred

 - Experience leading product initiatives from concept through launch

 - Strong experience working directly with Engineering teams in agile development environments

 - Ability to write clear product requirements and manage prioritization across competing initiatives

 - Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills

 - Excellent communication and stakeholder management abilities

 - Experience in legal technology, litigation technology, court data, or legal workflows

Preferred

 - Experience with search, analytics, and reporting products

 - Familiarity with billing systems, subscription management, and usage-based pricing models

 - Experience working with AP

source_jobs.title: Senior Account Executive- Central
source_jobs.description:
About Us

UniCourt is a Legal Data as a Service (LDaaS) company dedicated to unlocking the potential of court data for Am Law 200 firms and Fortune 500 companies. Delivered via the only API-first platform in the legal technology market, UniCourt provides real-time state and federal court data for legal analytics. Our proprietary technology turns disparate court records into accessible, actionable insights. The UniCourt Enterprise API is the gold standard in legal data delivery, powering data-driven revenue growth and client satisfaction strategies.

Position Summary

We're looking for a driven, relationship-oriented Account Executive to own and grow UniCourt's presence across the Central US market. This territory includes some of the fastest-growing legal markets in the country, with significant opportunity across large law firms headquartered or operating in the region.

You'll own the full sales cycle — from prospecting and pipeline development through negotiation and close — selling UniCourt's API-first legal data platform to large law firms and enterprises. The ideal candidate has deep familiarity with the Central US legal landscape, existing relationships within large law, and a track record of selling enterprise B2B data or legal tech solutions. You're comfortable engaging C-Suite and innovation leaders, and you know how to navigate complex, multi-stakeholder deals.

This is a true hunter role. You'll be expected to build pipeline, develop territory strategy, and drive new business while collaborating closely with marketing, product, and customer success.

Duties & Responsibilities

 - Own and execute a territory plan for the Central US, with a focus on large law firms across key metro areas such as Chicago, St. Louis, and Kansas City.

 - Prospect, qualify, and close new enterprise accounts within the territory.

 - Build and maintain relationships with C-Suite executives, knowledge management leaders, and innovation teams at target firms.

 - Employ a consultative sales approach to understand client needs and position UniCourt's data platform as a tailored solution.

 - Manage complex deal cycles involving implementation, integration, and data partners.

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with marketing, product, and sales leadership to refine messaging, share market intelligence, and inform go-to-market strategy.

 - Represent UniCourt at Central regional legal industry conferences, trade shows, and networking events.

 - Stay current on competitive landscape, market trends, and developments in the Southeast legal market.

 - Drive individual sales targets while contributing to overall team objectives.

Education, Knowledge, Skills & Abilities

 - Bachelor's degree required; JD or MBA preferred.

 - 5+ years of enterprise B2B sales experience, ideally selling data solutions, APIs, or legal technology to large law firms.

 - Demonstrated experience and existing relationships within the Southeast US legal market strongly preferred.

 - Experience selling to knowledge management, innovation, business intelligence, or information departments within large law.

 - Familiarity with structured data solutions including APIs, MDM, CDP, BI tools, and data analytics platforms.

 - Proven ability to manage multi-stakeholder deal cycles with implementation or integration partners.

 - Excellent communication, presentation, negotiation, and closing skills.

 - Self-starter mentality with strong organizational skills and the ability to operate independently within a remote team.

 - Willingness to travel regularly within the Central territory.

Compensation

Base salary range: $100,000–$150,000, depending on experience and qualifications. This is a commission-eligible role, and on-target earnings will be discussed during the interview process. UniCourt is committed to transparent, equitable compensation practices and discusses compensation openly from the first conversation.

Benefits

Competitive compensation and benefits package in

notes: Mostly sales roles, with a focus on account executives. Some product management roles, but no explicit tech roles.
